title: Exothermic Polymerization Thermal Runaway Safety Monitor
description: Monitors an exothermic free-radical polymerisation reaction (e.g., styrene with AIBN initiator) for thermal runaway. A DS18B20 array tracks bulk temperature; a software dT/dt detector flags runaway onset (> 0.5 °C/s or T > 90 °C); an SSR-Controlled Water Cooling Jacket activates automatically at 70 °C warning; a hardware dual-threshold relay interlock cuts power at 90 °C independently of the MCU. An OLED shows live temperature, rate, and fault state.
objective: Detect polymerisation runaway onset within 1 s, automatically engage cooling at warning threshold, and trigger hardware interlock at fault threshold with OLED and Serial fault reporting.
constraints: Arduino Uno, DS18B20 × 2, SSR cooling jacket, dual-threshold hardware interlock, OLED display; no more than 50 mL monomer per run; max safe temperature 90 °C; ventilated fume cupboard required.
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Two probes (core and surface) sampled at 0.5 Hz for spatial temperature gradient and runaway detection.
element_usage_notes.SSR-Controlled Water Cooling Jacket: Activated at 70 °C (WARNING) to remove reaction heat; duty cycle proportional to temperature excess.
element_usage_notes.Dual-Threshold Safety Relay Interlock: Hardware-only protection: cuts heater relay at 90 °C regardless of MCU state.
element_usage_notes.OLED Status Display: Shows T_core, T_surface, dT/dt, cooling duty, and FAULT / WARNING / OK banner.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop regulates temperature rate of rise; inner loop adjusts cooling jacket duty cycle.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Temperature readings feed outer rate-of-rise loop and inner temperature loop

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output sets SSR duty cycle to modulate cooling water flow
Analog thermistor mirror feeds hardware comparators for MCU-independent fault trip
Hardware fault state read back via digital pin and displayed on OLED
